summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #241: the `Editable` framework-agnostic core component in `ars-components`, following the component spec and issue comment's IME composition requirements, with no Leptos/Dioxus adapter work.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented `ars_components::input::editable` with state machine, IME composition handling, 13 committed insta snapshots, spec-conformance tests, proptest invariant tests, and 0 mutation survivors (133 caught, 15 unviable out of 148). Committed and pushed a non-draft PR targeting main with `Closes #241`.

repo: New input components follow the `text_field`/`textarea`/`combobox` state-machine pattern: `State`, `Event`, `Context`, `Props`, `Messages`, `Machine`, `Part`, `Api<'a>` in one `mod.rs` under `crates/ars-components/src/input/<name>/`.
`ComponentPart` derive already provides basic trait impls for `Part`; adding duplicate derives is a compile error, not a warning.
Snapshots live at `crates/ars-components/src/input/<component>/snapshots/` and are committed; `INSTA_UPDATE=always` is the accepted workflow for first-recording.
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` must target the full crate, not a filtered subset — a filter causes all non-matching snapshots to appear unreferenced and fails the check.
Post-implementation audit is a mandatory repo skill and covers spec/code drift, adapter specs, manifest/catalog surfaces, dependency surfaces, and test-coverage completeness before any commit.

Adapter specs (`spec/leptos-components/`, `spec/dioxus-components/`) must be kept in sync with the core contract — they had stale event names (`Confirm`, `InputChange`, `SetDisabled`) that were fixed as part of this work.
GitHub project board item IDs must be discovered via `gh project item-list` + `--jq` before `gh project item-edit` can move an issue to In Progress.
`cargo mutants -p ars-components -f <file>` is the required mutation gate; it runs ~23–26 minutes and must be run twice: once to find survivors, once to verify all gaps are closed.
Tab key submits while editing regardless of `submit_mode` per spec §3 — the initial implementation incorrectly tied it to the blur-submit mode, caught during the post-implementation spec drift audit.

code.finding: Reference pattern for `ComponentPart` derive and `Part` enum; shows the minimum needed without duplicate trait impls.
Tab key behavior spec: Tab submits unconditionally while editing, independent of `submit_mode`. Was not reflected in the initial implementation.

IME composition guard: `on_input_change` suppresses `Change` while `is_composing`; `on_input_keydown_after_composition_check` retries Enter only after the adapter's microtask resolves composition state.

workflow: TDD order: add failing spec-conformance test first (compile error is the red), then the production module, then unit tests before implementing transitions.
`cargo +nightly fmt --all` must run after all code changes and before the final clippy pass — formatting changes can introduce new clippy findings.
Private helper functions should be `const fn` by convention; clippy with `-D warnings` catches this but only after formatting is clean.
`cargo llvm-cov report --text --output-path /tmp/cov.txt` then grep for `\^0` is the practical way to find uncovered branches in a specific module.
Mutation survivors cluster by type: builder-unsetter methods, boolean guard inversions, per-prop sync detectors — adding one test per cluster type is more efficient than one test per survivor.
Running `cargo mutants` twice (find survivors → add tests → verify clean) is required even when the source changes are style-only, because the mutation baseline must match current source.

friction: `cargo insta test` filter semantics: passing a filter with `--unreferenced=reject` marks every non-matching snapshot as unreferenced, requiring a full-crate run instead of a scoped one.
Mutation runs take 23–26 minutes each; two full passes were required (first pass found 37 survivors, second pass confirmed 0), making mutation the longest gate by far.

GitHub project board update required three exploratory API calls (`gh project list`, `gh project field-list`, `gh project item-list`) to discover the opaque GraphQL IDs before the actual `item-edit` could proceed.
`cargo llvm-cov` inline test output does not include annotated per-file branch data; `--output-path` with a separate report step is required to see `^0` markers.
Nightly formatter changed code after clippy was already clean, forcing a second clippy run and minor patch for an induced style issue.
The `ComponentPart` duplicate-derive failure was not surfaced until the first compile — the convention is only discoverable by reading existing modules, not from error messages alone.
open_items: Leptos adapter component for Editable is explicitly out of scope — future work tracked in `spec/leptos-components/input/editable.md`.
Dioxus adapter component for Editable is explicitly out of scope — future work tracked in `spec/dioxus-components/input/editable.md`.
DOM-level adapter responsibilities deferred: focus management, text selection, caret placement, native element refs, and Firefox microtask scheduling for `on_input_keydown_after_composition_check`.
Proptest coverage was assessed as non-additive given unit test coverage of the small state surface; document decision should be revisited if the state machine grows.
